message:
So there I was, mid-hurricane warning, frantically texting my sister to confirm her storm plan—and my phone just… died. Not low battery. Dead. And of course my charger was in the car, which was already packed for evacuation.

Turned out fine (charged up at a friend's house), but wow did that moment hit different. Now I keep a little backup power bank in my emergency kit, and more importantly, I actually *wrote down* important phone numbers. You know, on actual paper. Like it's 1995.

Feels silly until the moment you need it, right?

Do you keep any old-school backup info handy, or am I the only one rediscovering pen and paper?
